name: Status Bar
description: Terminal-style status line with real-time indicators, mode display, and clickable sections.

top.description: Status bar at top of screen (title bar style)

bottom.description: Status bar at bottom of screen (vim style)

visual_brief: Fixed horizontal bar (top or bottom of viewport) with three alignment zones: left, center, and right. Each zone contains clickable status items rendered in monospace text with optional colored icons. Typical left items: mode indicator (INSERT/NORMAL), file name. Center items: line/column numbers, selection count. Right items: language mode, encoding, line endings, notification count. Background is a solid dark or accent color. Clicking a status item triggers a quick action or popup. The bar is a single row, densely packed with small text.

responsive.mobile: Bar shows only essential items (mode, file name, line/col). Other items accessible via tap-to-expand. Font size slightly reduced.
responsive.tablet: Most items visible. Right-side items may overflow to a menu.
responsive.desktop: Full bar with all items visible in left, center, and right zones.

layout_hints.zone_priority: Left, center, and right zones should remain stable and purposeful. Users should learn where to look for mode, context, and status without re-reading the bar every time.
layout_hints.density: Keep the bar terse and information-rich. Each status item should earn its place and avoid reading like a decorative label cloud.
layout_hints.overflow_strategy: On narrow widths, preserve critical mode and file context first and collapse the least essential items.

motion.micro: Live status changes may brighten or pulse subtly, and clickable items may sharpen on hover or focus.
motion.transitions: Realtime updates should feel stable and instrumentation-like rather than flashy or banner-like.

keyboard: Status items that open menus or actions should be reachable in order without disrupting the primary workflow.
announcements: Mode, position, encoding, and other state changes should be exposed semantically so the bar remains informative beyond visual placement.
